heading: Coordination of United States and Virgin Islands income taxes
label: 932 Coordination of United States and Virgin Islands income taxes
notes: Prior ProvisionsA prior section 932, acts , ; , , ; , , (3), ; , , , related to income taxation of citizens of possessions of the United States, prior to repeal by , , .Aug. 16, 1954, ch. 73668A Stat. 292Pub. L. 89–809, title I, § 103(m)80 Stat. 1554Pub. L. 92–606, § 1(f)(2)86 Stat. 1497Pub. L. 99–272, title XII, § 12103(a)100 Stat. 285Pub. Amendments2004—Subsecs. (a)(1)(A)(i), (c)(1)(A), (4)(A). substituted “during the entire taxable year” for “at the close of the taxable year”.Pub. L. 108–3571988—Subsec. (c)(2). , substituted “an income tax return” for “his income tax return”.Pub. L. 100–647, § 1012(w)(3)Subsec. (c)(4). , amended par. (4) generally. Prior to amendment, par. (4) read as follows: “In the case of an individual who is a bona fide resident of the Virgin Islands at the close of the taxable year and who, on his return of income tax to the Virgin Islands, reports income from all sources and identifies the source of each item shown on such return, for purposes of calculating income tax liability to the United States gross income shall not include any amount included in gross income on such return.”Pub. L. 100–647, § 1012(w)(2)Subsec. (e). , substituted current heading for “Section not to apply to tax imposed in Virgin Islands” and amended text generally. Prior to amendment, text read as follows: “This section shall not apply for purposes of determining income tax liability incurred to the Virgin Islands.”Pub. L. 100–647, § 1012(w)(1)

Regulations, , , as amended by , , , provided that:Pub. L. 99–514, title XII, § 1274(c)100 Stat. 2598Pub. L. 100–647, title I, § 1012(w)(4)102 Stat. 3530Oct. 22, 1986Nov. 10, 1988“The Secretary of the Treasury or his delegate shall prescribe such regulations as may be necessary or appropriate for applying the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 [this title] for purposes of determining tax liability incurred to the Virgin Islands.”

Authority To Impose Nondiscriminatory Local Income Taxes, , , provided that:Pub. L. 99–514, title XII, § 1274(b)100 Stat. 2597Oct. 22, 1986“Nothing in any provision of Federal law shall prevent the Virgin Islands from imposing on any person nondiscriminatory local income taxes. Any taxes so imposed shall be treated in the same manner as State and local income taxes under section 164 of the Internal Revenue Code of 1954 [now 1986] and shall not be treated as taxes to which section 901 of such Code applies.”

children.heading: Treatment of United States residents
children.label: a Treatment of United States residents
i
children.text: is a citizen or resident of the United States (other than a bona fide resident of the Virgin Islands during the entire taxable year), and

children.label: ii
children.text: has income derived from sources within the Virgin Islands, or effectively connected with the conduct of a trade or business within such possession, for the taxable year, or

children.label: B
children.text: such individual files a joint return for the taxable year with an individual described in subparagraph (A).

children.heading: Filing requirement
children.label: 2 Filing requirement
children.text: Each individual to whom this subsection applies for the taxable year shall file his income tax return for the taxable year with both the United States and the Virgin Islands.

children.heading: Extent of income tax liability
children.label: 3 Extent of income tax liability
children.text: In the case of an individual to whom this subsection applies in a taxable year for purposes of so much of this title (other than this section and section 7654) as relates to the taxes imposed by this chapter, the United States shall be treated as including the Virgin Islands.

children.heading: Portion of United States tax liability payable to the Virgin Islands
children.label: b Portion of United States tax liability payable to the Virgin Islands

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Each individual to whom subsection (a) applies for the taxable year shall pay the applicable percentage of the taxes imposed by this chapter for such taxable year (determined without regard to paragraph (3)) to the Virgin Islands.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: For purposes of paragraph (1), the term “applicable percentage” means the percentage which Virgin Islands adjusted gross income bears to adjusted gross income.

children.heading: Virgin Islands adjusted gross income
children.label: B Virgin Islands adjusted gross income
children.text: For purposes of subparagraph (A), the term “Virgin Islands adjusted gross income” means adjusted gross income determined by taking into account only income derived from sources within the Virgin Islands and deductions properly apportioned or allocable thereto.

children.heading: Amounts paid allowed as credit
children.label: 3 Amounts paid allowed as credit
children.text: There shall be allowed as a credit against the tax imposed by this chapter for the taxable year an amount equal to the taxes required to be paid to the Virgin Islands under paragraph (1) which are so paid.

children.heading: Treatment of Virgin Islands residents
children.label: c Treatment of Virgin Islands residents
A
children.text: such individual is a bona fide resident of the Virgin Islands during the entire taxable year, or

children.label: B
children.text: such individual files a joint return for the taxable year with an individual described in subparagraph (A).

children.heading: Filing requirement
children.label: 2 Filing requirement
children.text: Each individual to whom this subsection applies for the taxable year shall file an income tax return for the taxable year with the Virgin Islands.

children.heading: Extent of income tax liability
children.label: 3 Extent of income tax liability
children.text: In the case of an individual to whom this subsection applies in a taxable year for purposes of so much of this title (other than this section and section 7654) as relates to the taxes imposed by this chapter, the Virgin Islands shall be treated as including the United States.

children.label: A
children.text: who is a bona fide resident of the Virgin Islands during the entire taxable year,

children.label: B
children.text: who, on his return of income tax to the Virgin Islands, reports income from all sources and identifies the source of each item shown on such return, and

children.label: C
children.text: who fully pays his tax liability referred to in section 934(a) to the Virgin Islands with respect to such income,

children.heading: Special rule for joint returns
children.label: d Special rule for joint returns
children.text: In the case of a joint return, this section shall be applied on the basis of the residence of the spouse who has the greater adjusted gross income (determined without regard to community property laws) for the taxable year.

children.heading: Special rule for applying section to tax imposed in Virgin Islands
children.label: e Special rule for applying section to tax imposed in Virgin Islands
children.text: In applying this section for purposes of determining income tax liability incurred to the Virgin Islands, the provisions of this section shall not be affected by the provisions of Federal law referred to in section 934(a).
